intended_use: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of contrast-enhanced chest CTs (not dedicated CTPA protocol) in adults or transitional ado
intended_use_full: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of contrast-enhanced chest CTs (not dedicated CTPA protocol) in adults or transitional adolescents age 18 and older. The device is intended to assist hospital networks and appropriately trained medical specialists in workflow triage by flagging and communication of suspect cases of incidental Pulmonary Embolism (iPE) pathologies. The device is intended to be used on single-energy exams only.
ground_truth: Not explicitly stated; implied to be radiologist assessment or clinical reference standard for iPE diagnosis
other_metrics: Positive Likelihood Ratio: 9.09 (95% CI: 4.69, 17.62); Negative Likelihood Ratio: 0.11 (95% CI: 0.06, 0.22); Time to notification: 4.7 minutes (compared to 223.3 minutes for standard of care time-to-exam-open)
